rationale.R1.1: Thesis, gap statement, and contribution claim are all present but interleaved non-linearly; improving the draft centrally involves sharpening how these are articulated and sequenced.
rationale.R1.2: The logical chain (contamination → reward-model bias → reweighting remedy → empirical gain → theoretical guarantee) spans multiple paragraphs; checking premise-to-conclusion coherence and that the ablation supports the causal claim is central to any substantive improvement.
rationale.R1.3: Concrete quantitative claims (7.4-point win-rate lift, 18% reward-hacking reduction, ρ > 0.3 bound, 33–46% contamination rate) appear without table/figure anchors; alignment between these numbers and their described evidence sources is relevant to improving the draft.
rationale.R1.5: The draft's section order is motivation-last (method → results → motivation → theory → prior work), which inverts the standard IMRaD arc; structural reorganization is plausibly load-bearing for the improvement task.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is the load-bearing default interpretation of 'improve this draft'; sentence clarity, paragraph cohesion, and register consistency are primary deliverables regardless of other scope.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement be limited to sentence- and paragraph-level writing polish, or does it also include restructuring the argument's narrative order (motivation currently follows results) and/or surfacing logical or evidence gaps in the claims?
